tldr: Inference pipeline for scientific image forgery detection combining a frozen DINOv2 encoder with a custom decoder, enhanced by Error Level Analysis (ELA) and gradient-based adaptive thresholding to generate RLE-encoded segmentation masks.
summary: This notebook implements an inference pipeline for the Scientific Image Forgery Detection competition. It loads a pretrained DINOv2-base model (frozen) paired with a lightweight convolutional decoder to produce per-pixel forgery probability maps. The core innovation is integrating Error Level Analysis (ELA) by re-compressing images at JPEG quality 90, computing compression artifacts, and fusing the ELA heatmap and ELA-model predictions with the original RGB predictions. Post-processing uses Sobel gradient enhancement, Gaussian smoothing, and morphological operations with adaptive thresholding to finalize masks. The pipeline outputs RLE-encoded annotations for submission.
extensive_summary:
## Scientific Image Forgery Detection with ELA + DINOv2 Hybrid
This notebook provides a complete inference recipe for detecting forged scientific images. It leverages a pretrained DINOv2-base encoder (frozen) and a custom lightweight decoder (`DinoTinyDecoder`) to generate segmentation masks. The pipeline is enhanced with Error Level Analysis (ELA) to highlight compression artifacts typical of image manipulations.

## Model Architecture
The `DinoSegmenter` wraps a frozen `AutoModel` (DINOv2-base) and a `DinoTinyDecoder`. The decoder consists of three sequential convolutional blocks (768→384→192→96 channels) with ReLU activations and Dropout2d(0.1), followed by a 1x1 convolution to output 1 channel. Features from DINOv2 are reshaped from `(B, N, C)` to `(B, C, s, s)` and progressively upsampled via bilinear interpolation to 74x74, 148x148, 296x296, and finally the target size (518x518).

## Error Level Analysis (ELA) Integration
ELA is computed by re-compressing the input image at JPEG quality 90, calculating the absolute difference between the original and recompressed images, and scaling the result to [0, 255]. A grayscale heatmap is derived from the maximum channel of the ELA result. The pipeline fuses three signals: the RGB model prediction, the ELA model prediction (weighted 0.7), and the direct ELA heatmap (weighted 0.3), controlled by `ELA_WEIGHT=0.3`.

## Post-Processing & Thresholding
Predictions are refined using `enhanced_adaptive_mask`: Sobel gradients are computed, normalized, and blended with the probability map (`alpha_grad=0.45`). A Gaussian blur `(3,3)` is applied, followed by an adaptive threshold (`mean + 0.3 * std`). Morphological closing `(5,5)` and opening `(3,3)` clean the mask. Final classification uses `AREA_THR=200` and `MEAN_THR=0.22` on the resized mask. Masks are RLE-encoded for submission.

## Inference & TTA
The default pipeline runs without TTA (`USE_TTA=False`). When enabled, it applies horizontal and vertical flips, averages predictions, and flips them back. Input images are resized to 518x518, normalized to [0,1], and passed through the DINOv2 processor (clamped to 0-255, converted to byte, permuted to HWC). The notebook concludes by iterating over the test set, generating masks, and saving a CSV submission.
preprocessing: DINOv2 AutoImageProcessor (clamp 0-255, byte conversion, HWC permutation)
ELA re-compression at JPEG quality 90 with absolute difference scaling
ensemble: Combines RGB model predictions, ELA model predictions (70% weight), and direct ELA heatmap signals (30% weight) into a single probability map.
post_processing: Sobel gradient enhancement blended with probability map, Gaussian blur (3x3), adaptive thresholding (mean + 0.3*std), morphological closing (5x5) and opening (3x3), area/mean thresholds for classification, RLE encoding for masks.
notable_techniques: ELA re-compression at JPEG 90 to expose compression history anomalies
Gradient-enhanced adaptive thresholding using Sobel operators
Fusing raw ELA heatmap with model predictions via weighted averaging
Frozen DINOv2 encoder with lightweight bilinear upsampling decoder for segmentation
RLE encoding for compact mask submission
